64 Yoginis from Vajrayana Tibetan Tengyur text

# 64 Yoginis from Vajrayana Tibetan Tengyur text

[](https://substack.com/@tseringdorji)

![Tsering Dorji's avatar](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!VB0T!,w_36,h_36,c_fill,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Faedf658f-bb6c-4511-9227-fcdbd4e7c7bd_768x773.jpeg)

[Tsering Dorji](https://substack.com/@tseringdorji)

Dec 13, 2022

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

8

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

Share

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!8WxC!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fbucketeer-e05bbc84-baa3-437e-9518-adb32be77984.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F4796a956-a3c4-42ca-859c-c41cddbd6da4_351x535.jpeg)

![](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!8WxC!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fbucketeer-e05bbc84-baa3-437e-9518-adb32be77984.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F4796a956-a3c4-42ca-859c-c41cddbd6da4_351x535.jpeg)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

Fig. 1. དབང་མོ་ (Indrāṇī/Śaci): holds an arrow and a vajra in her two right hands, a bell and a bow in her two left hands. Her mount is an elephant. Image: origin Nepal, (15th century) Rubin Museum.

Text within this block will maintain its original spacing when published

``` text अष्ट अष्ट चतुःषष्टिः योगिनी བརྒྱད་བརྒྱད་དྲུག་ཅུ་རྩ་བཞིའི་རྣལ་འབྱོར་མ་ 
Eight Eight Sixty-four yoginis

Configuration of eight times eight, sixty-four yoginis:

There are eight lotuses with eight petals in the eight quarters. On each of the eight petals of the eight lotuses, sits the sixty-four yoginis. 
On the centre (anthers) of the eight lotuses sits the chief eight yoginis surrounded by other eight respective yoginis. 
```

Text within this block will maintain its original spacing when published

``` text The eight chief yoginis in the eight directions: 

1. Eastern: Yogini Carcikā (ཙཱ་རྩི་ཀཱ་). 
2. South-Eastern (མེའི་མཚམས་/Skt. Agnikoṇa): Yogini Vaiṣṇavī (ཁྱབ་འཇུག་མ་). 
3. South-Western (Tib. བདེན་བྲལ་/Skt. Nairṛtya): Yogini Vārāhī (ཕག་མོ་). 
4. Southern: Yogini ṣaṇmukhī (གདོང་དྲུག་མ་). 
5. Northern (uttare): Yogini Raudrī śuklā  (དྲག་མོ་དཀར་མོ) 6. North-Eastern (དབང་ལྡན/Skt.aiśānī): Yogini lakṣmī (དཔལ་མོ་ ). 7. North-Western (རླུང་/Skt. Vāyavya): Yogini Brahmāṇī (ཚངས་མ་ ) 8. Western (direction of god Varuna/ཆུ་ལྷར་): Yogini Śaci དབང་མོ་ (Indra's consort). ```

List of Sixty-four Yoginis:

1.  ཙཱ་རྩི་ཀཱ་ (Carcikā): She holds a sword and trident in her two right
    hands, skull cup and khaṭvāṅga in her two left hands.

2.  ཁྱབ་འཇུག་མ་ (vaiṣṇavī): She holds a staff and a wheel in her two right
    hands, lotus and spear in her two left hands. Her mount is Garuda.

    [](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!iP84!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fbucketeer-e05bbc84-baa3-437e-9518-adb32be77984.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F3ab9d321-b03c-45a2-a503-cbaabb484d63_392x535.jpeg)
    ![](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!iP84!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fbucketeer-e05bbc84-baa3-437e-9518-adb32be77984.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F3ab9d321-b03c-45a2-a503-cbaabb484d63_392x535.jpeg)
    ![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)
    ![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

    Fig. 2. ཁྱབ་འཇུག་མ་ (vaiṣṇavī): She holds a staff and a wheel in her
    two right hands, lotus and spear in her two left hands. Her mount is
    Garuda. Image: origin Nepal, (17th century) HAR.

3.  ཕག་མོ་ (Vārāhī): holds a staff and a sword in her two right hands,
    armour and an iron chain in her left hand. Her mount is buffalo.

4.  གཞོན་ནུ་མ་ (Kumārī): holds a spear and an iron hook in her two right
    hands, jewel and noose in her left hand. Her mount is a peacock.

    [](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!rnCq!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fbucketeer-e05bbc84-baa3-437e-9518-adb32be77984.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Fe2bbce66-8863-429e-b426-a1f94599f7ce_353x535.jpeg)
    ![](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!rnCq!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fbucketeer-e05bbc84-baa3-437e-9518-adb32be77984.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Fe2bbce66-8863-429e-b426-a1f94599f7ce_353x535.jpeg)
    ![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)
    ![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

    Fig. 3. གཞོན་ནུ་མ་ (Kumārī): holds a spear and an iron hook in her two
    right hands, jewel and noose in her left hand. Her mount is a
    peacock. Image: origin Nepal, (15th century) Rubin Museum.

5.  དབང་མོ་ (Indrāṇī/Śaci): holds an arrow and a vajra in her two right
    hands, a bell and a bow in her two left hands. Her mount is an
    elephant.

6.  ཚངས་མ་ (Brahmāṇī): holds a lotus and a Brahma’s stick (brahmadaṇḍaḥ)
    in her two right hands, a pitcher and a cup in her two left hands.
    Her mount is a swan.

7.  དྲག་མོ་ (Raudrī): holds a trident and a damaru in her two right hands,
    a khaṭvāṅga and a snake in her two left hands. Her mount is a bull.

8.  བཀྲ་ཤིས་ཆེན་མོ་ (Mahalakshmi/Mangala): holds a lotus, a jewel and so
    forth. Her mount is a lion.

9.  འཇིག་བྱེད་མ་ (Bhairavī).

10. མཆེ་བ་གཉིས་གཙིགས་མ་

11. ཁ་ནས་མེ་འབར་མ་ (Jvālāmukhī)

12. རླུང་ཤུགས་མ་ (Vāyuvegā)

13. རབ་གཏུམ་མ་ (pracaṇḍā)

14. དྲག་མིག་མ་ (raudrākṣī krodhadevī)

15. སྣ་ཆེན་མ་ (sthūlanāsā/ Vaināyakī?)

16. དཔལ་མོ་ (lakshmi/sri devi)

17. སྒྱུ་འཕྲུལ་མ་ (māyādevi)

18. གྲགས་མ་ (Kīrti)

19. བཀྲ་ཤིས་མ་ (lakshmi?)

20. ཤིན་ཏུ་ང་རྒྱལ་མ་ (Manada?)

21. དཔལ་རྒྱལ་མ་ (śrījayā)

22. དཔལ་ཅན་མ་ (

23. འཁོར་ལོ་ཅན་མ་ (cakrī)

24. ཀེང་རུས་མ་ (kaṅkālī)

25. དུས་མཚན་མ་ (kālarātrī)

26. ཁྲོ་གདོང་མ་ (kupitavadanā)

27. དུས་ལྕེ་མ་ (kālajihvā)

28. གཙིགས་མ་ (karālī)

29. ནག་མོ་ (Kālī)

30. གཟུགས་ངན་མ་ (virūpā)

31. པདྨ་ (Padma)

32. ཡན་ལག་མེད་མ་ (anaṅgā)

33. རི་དྭགས་བདག་མོ་ (mṛgādhipatiḥ?)

34. རིན་ཆེན་ཕྲེང་མ་ (Ratnamālā)

35. མིག་བཟང་མ་ (sunetrā)

36. རུལ་པ་མ་ (klinnā)

37. བཟང་མོ་ (

38. རྡོ་རྗེ་འོད་མ་ (vajrābhā)

39. རྡོ་རྗེ་ལུས་མ་ (vajragātrā)

40. གསེར་གྱི་མཆོག་ལྡན་མ་

41. རིགས་འཛིན་མ་ (vidyādharī)

42. སྣ་ཚོགས་རི་མོ་འདྲི་མ་ (citralekhā)

43. ཆུ་ཤིང་མ་ (rambhā)

44. ཟུག་རྔུ་སེལ་མ་ (Pain soother)

45. ཤིན་ཏུ་སྒྲོལ་མ་ (su-tārā)

46. མཆོག་སྦྱིན་འདོན་མ་ (varadā)

47. པདྨའི་མིག་མ་ (padmanetrā)

48. ཆུ་སྐྱེས་ལྡན་མ་ (/jalakamini/Pamini?)

49. བློ་མ་

50. ངག་གི་དབང་ཕྱུག་མ་ (vāgīśvarī)

51. གླུ་ལེན་མ་ (gāyatrī)

52. གློག་མ་ (vidyut)

53. དྲན་པ་མ་ (Smṛti)

54. གཽ་རཱི་ (Gauri)

55. གཾ་གཱ་ (Gaṅgā)

56. རྟག་མ་ (Nityā)

57. ཤིན་ཏུ་མགྱོགས་མ་ (paramatvaritā)

58. འདེབས་མ་

59. སེར་སྐྱ་མ་ (piṅgalā)

60. དཔལ་ལྡན་དཀར་མོ་ (śrīśvetā)

61. ཟླ་བའི་རི་མོ་ཅན་ (candralekhā)

62. རི་བོང་འཛིན་ཞལ་མ་ (śaśadharavadanā)

63. ངང་པའི་མདོག་ཅན་ (haṃsavarṇā)

64. འཛིན་བྱེད་མ་ (vimalaśaśadharā)
